patch.value: GitHub Dependabot alert #3 reports arbitrary file read and execution when the Vitest UI server listens. The production runtime is not directly exposed, but contributor and CI environments must not retain a critical vulnerable dev dependency.
The resolved Vitest 1.6.1 is vulnerable to GHSA-5xrq-8626-4rwp and must be upgraded with its coverage provider.
Vitest and @vitest/coverage-v8 resolve to a patched matched version; full tests and coverage pass; GitHub alert closes after merge
Alert metadata, vulnerable range, patched floor, and current lock resolution are captured.
Inspect bun.lock or run bun pm ls vitest; observe 1.6.1 below patched 3.2.6.
No dependency resolves a vulnerable Vitest version.
Vitest 1.6.1 is committed and alert #3 is open.
Indirect supply-chain and contributor-environment risk; production dependency set is unaffected.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Source: GitHub security/dependabot/3, severity critical, CVE-2026-47429.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Upgrade coverage-v8 atomically with Vitest to preserve peer compatibility.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: The prior separate Dependabot PRs could not succeed because only one side of the Vitest/coverage pairing was updated.

author: codex-root

patch.value: bun.lock resolves Vitest 4.1.10, 542 tests and coverage gates pass, production audits report zero vulnerabilities, and GHSA-5xrq-8626-4rwp is fixed.
No dependency resolves a vulnerable Vitest version; tests and coverage remain green.
Upgraded Vitest and @vitest/coverage-v8 together to 4.1.10; GitHub marked Dependabot alert #3 fixed at 2026-07-16T17:13:49Z.
Patched and verified in merged PR #45

message: Close critical advisory after merged-head confirmation
